entry.title: Re: SAP Retail Pricing Function PRICE_CALCULATE_POSTING & VKP6
entry.content:
To replicate the price activation functionality of VKP6 and correctly populate internal tables like G_T_KOMV and G_T_KALP.
you need to follow a specific sequence of function modules and data preparation steps.

 Prepare Pricing Structures

Use the FM SPR_KOMK_KOMP_FILL to populate:

  • KOMK (Header pricing data)
  • KOMP (Item pricing data)

This is essential before calling any pricing-related FM.

 Populate Pricing Conditions

Manually or programmatically fill the internal table KOMV with relevant condition types and values. This is often done based on:

  • Material
  • Sales area
  • Pricing procedure

You can also use FM PRICING to calculate and populate KOMV based on KOMK and KOMP 

 Call Price Activation FM

Use PRICE_CALCULATE_POSTING to simulate the VKP6 activation. This FM expects:

  • G_T_KALP to be filled with item-level pricing data.
  • G_T_KOMV to contain calculated condition values.

If G_T_KOMV is empty, it means pricing was not calculated or the conditions were not fetched correctly.

CALL FUNCTION 'SPR_KOMK_KOMP_FILL'
EXPORTING
... "Pass material, org data, etc.
IMPORTING
E_KOMK = ls_komk
E_KOMP = ls_komp.

CALL FUNCTION 'PRICING'
EXPORTING
KOMK = ls_komk
KOMP = ls_komp
TABLES
KOMV = lt_komv.

" Now lt_komv contains pricing conditions
MOVE lt_komv TO G_T_KOMV.
MOVE item data TO G_T_KALP.

CALL FUNCTION 'PRICE_CALCULATE_POSTING'
... " With G_T_KOMV and G_T_KALP filled

  • Ensure that BPSTA is updated correctly to reflect status change from A to C.
  • You may need to simulate the pricing procedure determination if calling from a Fiori app.
  • Consider wrapping this logic in a custom RFC for clean integration with your app.

entry.title: Re: HANA Memory KPI used in CALM not same as SAP4Me
entry.content:
Hello @lvbarat 

This is a great question, and it often arises when comparing system metrics across different SAP monitoring tools.

The difference you're seeing is expected due to how and when each tool collects memory usage. CALM reflects real-time application-level usage, while SAP for Me gives a periodic snapshot of SAP's backend systems, which may include buffer or reserved memory. For technical monitoring and live troubleshooting, CALM is more accurate. For licence and consumption tracking, SAP for Me is the correct reference.

This Illustrates the Difference

Source

CALM (Cloud ALM)

SAP for Me

Metric Type

Real-time operational memory usage

Backend snapshot for licensing/quota

Data Frequency

Dynamic; near-real-time via monitoring services

Periodic, aggregated meter

Content

Includes buffers, runtime caches, monitoring data

Includes reserved capacity, contract-level metrics

 

Hope it helps

Thank you and kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: The client is locked for data import by client copy
entry.content:
Hello,

Tcode SCC1, SCC1N usage will be refrained into system due to below setting into Tcode SCC4 -

Client Copy & Comparison Tool Protection 

Please make it to - Protection Level 0: No restriction to enable it.

Tcode SCC1N has been introduced from SAP_BASIS 755.

Hence your system's client can be locked for cross client and repository objects and you need to review above setting to enable Tcode SCC1, SCC1N.

Best Regards,

Rupali

entry.title: Re: Alation HANA DB Connector in violation of HANA Runtim...
entry.content:
I can tell you about Collibra because I am using it.
You connect the source systems you want to DSP, and in the DSP catalog it creates the assets that represent the different objects (analytical models, views, tables).

Collibra connects to the DSP catalog (not to the source systems connected to DSP) and extracts the information from these assets. There is no odbc access to any site. It will not see anything that is not in the DSP catalog because as I said it does not access the source, only what is published in the DSP catalog.

Best regards.

entry.title: Handle "MaxListenersExceededWarning" which calling multiple external API's in SAP CAPM
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are doing a quarterly cleanup of shadow users from BTP cockpit based on the last logon date. We are using the XSUAA API to fetch the users from a Subaccount. As we have multiple subaccounts I have called the Central Information Service to get the list of Subaccounts and then for all subaccounts I fetch the users from each subaccount. Sequence is:

  1. Fetch all subaccounts using cds.connect.to('subaccountdest')
  2. Fetch users in subaccounts via promise all for parallel processing calling each subaccount via cds.connect.to('dynamincssubacdest')

I have the below code:

async function getXsuaaConnection(subdomain) {
    const dest = `xsuaa-api-${subdomain}`;
    try {
        if (!xsuaaConnections[dest]) {
            xsuaaConnections[dest] = await cds.connect.to(dest);
        }
        return xsuaaConnections[dest];
    } catch (err) {
        console.error(`❌ Failed to connect to destination ${dest}:`, err.message);
        throw new Error(`Connection to ${dest} failed`);
    }
}

async function getUsersNew(xsuaa, saDomain, saID, saName) {
    try {
        const xsuaaResp = await xsuaa.get('/Users?count=500');
        const xsuaaUsers = xsuaaResp.resources || [];
        return {
            users: xsuaaUsers.map(user => ({
                userName: user.userName,
                externalId: user.externalId,
                btpId: user.id,
                firstName: user.name?.givenName,
                lastName: user.name?.familyName,
                displayName: `${user.name?.givenName || ''} ${user.name?.familyName || ''}`,
                eMail: user.emails?.[0]?.value || null,
                origin: user.origin,
                isActive: user.active,
                isVerified: user.verified,
                lastLogon: getDate(user.lastLogonTime),
                parent_ID: saID,
                subName: saName,
                subDomain: saDomain,
                createdOn: user.meta?.created
            }))
        };
    } catch (err) {
        console.error(`❌ Failed to fetch users for subdomain ${saDomain}:`, err.message);
        return { users: [] };
    }
}

async function getSa() {
    try {
        const subAccResp = await cis.get('/accounts/v1/subaccounts');
        let subAccs = Array.isArray(subAccResp?.value) ? subAccResp.value : [];

        subAccs = subAccs.filter(sa =>
            sa.subdomain !== 'appdev' && sa.subdomain !== 'ext-portal'
        );

        const subAccounts = subAccs.map(sa => ({
            id: sa.guid,
            name: sa.displayName,
            region: sa.region,
            subDomain: sa.subdomain,
            desc: sa.description
        }));

        const userResults = await Promise.all(
            subAccs.map(async sa => {
                try {
                    const xsuaa = await getXsuaaConnection(sa.subdomain);
                    return await getUsersNew(xsuaa, sa.subdomain, sa.guid, sa.displayName);
                } catch (err) {
                    console.error(`⚠️ Skipping subaccount ${sa.subdomain}:`, err.message);
                    return { users: [] };
                }
            })
        );

        const users = userResults.flatMap(r => r.users);
        return { subaccounts: subAccounts, users };
    } catch (err) {
        console.error('❌ Failed to fetch subaccounts:', err.message);
        return { subaccounts: [], users: [] };
    }
}

As we have more than 10 calls I get the below warning(In future we can have more calls as well) :

"MaxListenersExceededWarning: Possible EventEmitter memory leak detected. 11 succeeded listeners added to [EventEmitter]"

Presently I have increased the limit of Event Emitters and warning is gone, but I have read that this is not a best practice. 

Any help will be appreciated.

@qmacro 

Regards

Braham

entry.title: SAP XMII
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

As manufacturing professionals, many of us rely on SAP Manufacturing Integration and Intelligence (MII) to connect shop floor operations with our ERP systems. However, SAP has announced that support for SAP MII and SAP MES will end on December 31, 2030, extended from the original 2027 deadline. With only five years left, it’s time to plan our transition to avoid disruptions.

I’ve been researching SAP MII’s retirement and the options available, and I’d love to hear your thoughts. What’s your organization’s plan? Are you facing challenges or finding successes in this transition? Let’s share insights to navigate this change together!

entry.title: Re: Inquiry about Adapt UI on Dependent Fields in Customi...
entry.content:
Hello @Tiffany3 

Thank you for your question regarding UI adaptation in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. You’ve raised an important and common topic, especially as you approach go-live preparation.

Recommended Approach

Yes, it is fully acceptable and SAP-supported to temporarily create sample master data in the Customization Tenant (D/C) solely for the purpose of performing Adapt UI tasks — especially when the UI elements you need to modify are context-dependent and only become visible after entering certain data (e.g., a project profile or category).

This is a recognized best practice during the Fit-to-Standard and extensibility phase when adapting runtime-driven screens. Since UI adaptation is only possible on rendered fields, creating minimal data helps surface the correct screen state.

Best Practice Guidelines

  • Create lightweight “dummy” master data (e.g., a test project, sales doc, asset).
  • Use this data to access the desired screen state in the Project Control – Enterprise Projects app.
  • Perform your UI adjustments (label rename, visibility, grouping, etc.).
  • Delete or flag the test data after use (e.g., prefix with ZTEST_) — it is not transported.
  • Transport only the UI adaptation layer via the Export Software Collection app.

What to Avoid

  • Do not create large volumes or use real/production-like master data in the D/C tenant.
  • Ensure that the dummy data is not used in any configuration testing or migrated to T/P.

SAP Official Reference

SAP Note 2680324 “Adapt UI relies on screen context and runtime rendering of UI elements. Fields not visible at runtime cannot be adapted.”

We’re here to support a smooth and confident go live.

Warm regards,

Chuma

Regards


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: EWA Generation - SAP NetWeaver System w/o Solman
entry.content:
For AS JAVA System, Focused Run Or Solution Manager system is needed, to generate the EarlyWatch Alert report.
Only with Focused Run system, the JAVA EWA data can be transferred to the EWA application in SAP for Me.
For HANA database, if you run HANA native applications - this is a HANA system without an ABAP system on top - HANA Cockpit transfers the EWA data to SAP. HANA Cockpit is the right choice if you have no Solution Manager or Focused Run. 

For more details, please refer to the SAP note 1257308 - FAQ: Using EarlyWatch Alert

entry.title: Re: Currency conversion insufficient privilege
entry.content:
It is strange.

When creating currency conversion views in DEV and PRD (shared from SAP_CONTENT), both spaces have their own objects, like “SAP.CURRENCY.VIEW.TCURX”, etc.

Currency conversions created in DEV should use the DEV views (accessing to SAP_CONTENT tables), and when exported to PRD, they should access the ones in PRD (accessing to SAP_CONTENT tables), without problems because they use the same technical name in DEV and PRD.

If that is not the case, and you get the permissions failure, I would say it is because somehow from PRD it is trying to access the DEV currency conversion views, as if it “remembers” something like "DEV". "SAP.CURRENCY.VIEW.TCURX" instead of just ”SAP.CURRENCY.VIEW.TCURX" (withouth the space)

As a workaround, you could share those views from DEV to PRD to make that call work, but it doesn't seem very robust.

Another option, if you are using currency conversion in the graphical views, would be to do them with the CURRENCY_CONVERSION() function instead of the “wizard”, that should work like any other function and referencing the tables as “SAP.CURRENCY.VIEW.TCURX” should be transparent and work in PRD as well.

Regards,

entry.title: The growth of DSVASRESULTSGEN table
entry.content:
Solman本番機にて、DSVASRESULTSGENテーブルが月に１GB以上増加しており、ディスク領域が逼迫傾向にあります。
現在システム上で、EWAアーカイブ（RDARCH_ARCHIVE_DATA_SOLMAN）や削除（RDSMOPREDUCEDATA）などのレポートは定期実行されていません。

まずは以下確認させてください。
1) DSVASRESULTSGENテーブルが月に１GB以上増加するのは正常な動作でしょうか。
2) Note.2075483を確認しましたが、現在の状況においてはEWAアーカイブもしくは削除のジョブを実行することにより、
   この状況を改善することができるでしょうか。

On the Solman production system, the DSVASRESULTSGEN table is growing by more than 1GB per month, and disk space is becoming tight.
Currently, reports such as EWA archiving (RDARCH_ARCHIVE_DATA_SOLMAN) and deletion (RDSMOPREDUCEDATA) are not run regularly on the system.

Please let me first confirm the following.
1) Is it normal for the DSVASRESULTSGEN table to grow by more than 1GB per month?
2) I have checked Note.2075483, but in the current situation, would it be possible to improve this situation by running an EWA archiving or deletion job?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: The growth of DSVASRESULTSGEN table
entry.content:
＞1) DSVASRESULTSGENテーブルが月に１GB以上増加するのは正常な動作でしょうか。
→DSVASRESULTSGENテーブルはすべてのサービス セッション情報が含まれていますので、月に１GB以上増加するのは正常な動作です。

＞2) Note.2075483を確認しましたが、現在の状況においてはEWAアーカイブもしくは削除のジョブを実行することにより、この状況を改善することができるでしょうか。 

→はい、ご認識の通りです。
Solmanをご利用の際、ディスク枯渇の心配或いは問題がある場合は、まず下記KBAをご参照いただき、DBACockpitもしくはほかのデータベースツールからテーブルサイズのTop 10とサイズ増加スピードTop 10を特定し、
各々のテーブル に対し、ハウスキーピング作業を検討することをお推奨いたします。
2549260 - Certain tables in Solution Manager system grow fast

尚、ディスク枯渇現象が主としてDSVASRESULTSGENによる場合は、以下のノートに従えば改善できます。
2075483 - EarlyWatch Alert Archiving 
2427041 - How to delete old EWA sessions according to retention times automatically through a job 

どのぐらいのデータを保存することはお客様次第で、レポート実行中指定する必要で、お手数ですが、御社運用上の必要に応じて、ご決定お願いします。


As per 2549260 - Certain tables in Solution Manager system grow fast
The table contain service session information for all the sessions delivered in Solution Manager. Old sessions can be deleted via standard report RDSMOPREDUCEDATA.
See SAP Notes for more information.
2075483 - EarlyWatch Alert Archiving 
2427041 - How to delete old EWA sessions according to retention times automatically through a job

entry.title: Re: Allowance Creation and Activation Upon Subscription C...
entry.content:
Hi,
If you want to create the allowance automatically in CC during contract creation the following is needed:

  1. In the allowance definition group you must set the flag for contract creation.
  2. In the CCM mapping you must assign this allowance definition group.

When I understand you correctly, you missed step 2 and you assigned "Full Table". So, you should change your CCM mapping and then it should work.

Best regards,
Thomas

entry.title: Re: SAP VIM Available services/API: OTBCINB/PF01_REGIST_D...
entry.content:
Hi Delano,

entry.title: Re: MRM Blog 2: Building an Integration Flow with SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
There is a mistake in the groovy script. The double single quotes should be just one single and also the import of java.util.HashMap is not needed. Correct code would look like:

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message;
import groovy.json.JsonOutput;
import groovy.util.XmlParser;
import java.time.LocalDate;
import java.time.format.DateTimeFormatter;

def Message processData(Message message) {
    // Body
    def body = message.getBody(String.class);

    // Parse the XML data
    def parser = new XmlParser();
    def xml = parser.parseText(body);
    def date = xml.Cube.Cube[0].@time;

    // Increase the date by one day
    def formatter = DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern("yyyy-MM-dd");
    def originalDate = LocalDate.parse(date, formatter);
    def newDate = originalDate.plusDays(1).format(formatter); //because business logic in S4

    def rates = xml.Cube.Cube[0].Cube.collect { node ->
        [
            providerCode     : "Y001",
            marketDataSource : "BYOR",
            marketDataCategory: "01",
            key1             : "EUR",
            key2             : node.@currency,
            marketDataProperty: "CMID",
            effectiveDate    : newDate,
            effectiveTime    : "00:00:00",
            marketDataValue  : node.@rate.replace(',', '.').replace(' ', '').toDouble(),
            fromFactor       : "1",
            toFactor         : "1"
        ]
    }

    def jsonOutput = JsonOutput.toJson(rates);
    message.setBody(JsonOutput.prettyPrint(jsonOutput));

    message.setHeader('Content-Type', 'application/json');

    return message;
}

entry.title: Re: The use of Table J_1BBRANCH is not permitted in sap p...
entry.content:
Hi,

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition, direct access to tables like J_1BBRANCH is restricted, and you cannot use them directly in your ABAP code or as part of an inner join. This is due to the limitations imposed by the cloud environment to ensure security and stability.

Alternative Solutions

  1. Use Released CDS Views: Check if there are any released CDS views that expose the data you need from the J_1BBRANCH table. CDS views are the recommended way to access data in SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

  2. Custom CDS Views: SAP S/4HANA Cloud : Key Takeaways on CDS Views - SAP Community If a standard CDS view does not meet your requirements, you can create a custom CDS view using the Fiori app ‘Custom CDS Views’. This allows you to define your own logic and expose the necessary fields.

  3. API Usage: Explore if there are any APIs available that provide the GSTIN details you need. SAP S/4HANA Cloud offers a variety of APIs that can be used to access data.

Best regards, Dequan Xu

entry.title: United in purpose: ScottishPower and SAP at Future of Utilities
entry.content:
Last week, we had the incredible opportunity to join forces with our valued customer, ScottishPower, at the Future of Utilities Summit in London. 

Together, we showcased our shared vision for the future of customer experience for utilities—one that is customer-centric, intelligent, and cloud-powered. At the SAP Lounge and booth, attendees experienced firsthand how SAP’s technology and ScottishPower’s innovation are transforming the energy sector. Our team was having meaningful customer and partner conversations and was of course able to join one or the other session of our presenting customers.

One of the highlights of the event was the keynote presentation delivered by Andrew Ward, CEO of the Customer Business at ScottishPower and Daniela Haldy-Sellmann, Global VP at SAP.  Their session emphasized the power of collaboration and technology in delivering exceptional customer experiences.

Here are some of the key outcomes they shared:

  • 💵 38% reduction in back-office costs
  • 💻 95% fewer back-office interventions
  • 📱 4.8 / 5.0 average rating in app reviews
  • 📞 21% shorter call-handling times

These results are a testament to what’s possible when cutting-edge technology meets a clear customer vision. By leveraging a modern, AI-infused cloud platform, ScottishPower is not only optimizing operations but also building a brand that customers love and trust.

If you visited our booth, we hope you enjoyed the deep dives into our joint solutions. If not, we’d love to continue the conversation—because the future of utilities is being written today, and we’re proud to be writing it together.

A heartfelt thank you to the ScottishPower team for making our first joint presence at this event such a success!

We’re excited about what lies ahead and look forward to continuing this journey of innovation and impact—together.

entry.title: Re: Strategy Group 55: PIRs Consumption with reference to...
entry.content:
Hello

This could occur due to incorrect or missing configuration in the requirements consumption logic. Here's what you should review:

 1. Check the Consumption Mode and Requirements Class

Go to transaction OVZI (Requirements Class):

  • Locate the Requirements Class associated with Strategy Group 55 (often class 102).
  • Ensure the "Consume PIRs" setting is set to require an exact configuration match.
  • Specifically, verify field "Requirement Cons. With Config." is set correctly:
    • It should be set to blank or a value that requires exact configuration match, depending on your system version.
    • In some systems, value '1' allows approximate match — avoid this if exact match is needed.

 2. Configuration Profile Settings (CU42)

For your configurable material and its variants, go to transaction CU42:

  • Make sure the configuration profile used does not allow flexible matching.
  • In particular:
    • Set "Planning Material" correctly for the variant.
    • If dependency logic (preconditions, selection conditions) is used, verify that it aligns with strict matching rules.

 3. PIR Planning Variant (CTPV)

You mentioned using a planning variant with assigned characteristics.

  • Double-check that the variant is properly assigned and active.
  • Run transaction CTPV (for planning variant maintenance) to review the exact values assigned.
  • Make sure there is no fallback behavior (e.g., missing value = ignore) that could allow 95% matching.

 4. Check the BAdI / Enhancement Use

If your system has custom enhancements, verify if BAdI MD_PIR_FLEX_CONS or MD_PIR_REDUCTION (or equivalent exits in customer namespace) have been implemented to allow consumption based on closest match.

You might need to adjust or deactivate this logic if it leads to unwanted consumption.

 5. Test with Simulation

Use transaction MD63/MD73 to simulate PIR consumption:

  • Create two PIRs for different characteristic values.
  • Create a sales order with a mismatched configuration.
  • Simulate requirements reduction and observe which PIR is consumed.

Recommended Fix Steps

  1. Review the Requirements Class (OVZI) and ensure the configuration consumption behavior is exact.
  2. Confirm planning variants are strictly assigned without optional fields or missing values.
  3. Check if any BAdIs or user exits are overriding standard behavior.
  4. If available, enable the check in your system to prevent PIR consumption when the configuration is not a 100% match (may vary slightly by enhancement pack/version).

Summary

  • Strategy 55 supports strict configuration-based PIR consumption.
  • If your system consumes PIRs for partial matches, it indicates a misconfigured requirement class, configuration profile, or planning variant, or possibly a custom enhancement allowing flexible match.
  • Correcting this ensures PIRs are only consumed when sales orders have an exact configuration match, as you intend.

Best regards

entry.title: Re: SAP PM PERMIT
entry.content:
Hello

Could you pls try this?

1. Implement Permit Extension if Work Duration Crosses 8 Hours or a Shift

To handle permit extension based on work duration:

  • Use user exit IWO10009 or enhancement spot to monitor the total time confirmed for the work order.
  • Implement logic that checks if confirmed hours exceed 8 hours or go beyond the shift.
  • If so, automatically trigger a workflow or alert to request an extension of the associated permit.
  • Optionally, update the permit status to "Extended" or create a new extended permit record with reference to the original.

2. Closure of Permit with Reasons (Work Completed, Online Sealing, Spare Required, etc.)

To track reasons for closing permits:

  • Add a custom field or use catalog codes (using PM notification catalog functionality) to allow users to select the reason for closure.
  • You can implement a popup or input field enhancement during permit closure to capture this.
  • Store this value in a Z-field or long text for auditing and reporting.
  • Optionally, configure a workflow that routes the closure reason to a supervisor or logs it automatically for analytics.

3. Make Work Order Type Mandatory in the Permit

To ensure that a work order type is linked to the permit:

  • Implement validation logic using BAdI BADI_EAM_PERMIT or user exit IPRM0001.
  • Check that the permit is always assigned to a work order and that this order contains a valid type (such as PM01, PM02, etc.).
  • You can enforce this via screen variant (transaction SHD0) to make the order type a mandatory input before permit can be saved or released.

Best regards

Another regular meeting would be a good idea around this year's SAP TechEd in Berlin in November. Please submit your suggestions.

I wish you a wonderful summer in Berlin or wherever you decide to spend it.

entry.title: Re: Document xxxxxx does not exist - Intercompany sales -...
entry.content:
Hello

 Root Cause

RD04 is used in the Intercompany Billing (IV invoice) scenario to post the Accounts Payable invoice in the purchasing company code.
However, RD04 expects some form of reference to create the FI document — most commonly a Purchase Order (PO).

If your process does not include an MM PO, but still uses RD04, this can cause the error because:

  • The IDoc (type INVOIC) looks for a document reference (usually from field E1EDK02 with qualifier “001” – Purchase Order).
  • If this reference is filled (e.g. with the SO PO number), but there’s no corresponding MM document in the receiving system, the posting fails.

 Clarifications for Your Process

You're doing:

  1. Sales Order (Company A to End Customer, with internal partner)
  2. Delivery
  3. End-customer Billing (Company A)
  4. Intercompany Billing (Company B to Company A)
  5. RD04 sends IDoc from SD Billing in Company B to create AP invoice in Company A (vendor: Company B)

In this case:

  • There is no PO, but RD04 still tries to post an MM invoice (MIRO-like logic) using the reference in the IDoc.
  • If the IDoc includes a PO number (e.g., copied from the Sales Order field), but no PO exists in Company A, the system will not find a match and gives the error: “Document xxxxxx does not exist.”

Solution Options

Option 1: Use FI Posting Instead of MM Posting

Change the processing logic of RD04 to post a direct FI invoice (FB60 style) instead of an MM invoice. This can be done by:

  • Adjusting partner profile (transaction WE20) for RD04 to trigger function module IDOC_INPUT_INVOIC_FI instead of IDOC_INPUT_INVOIC_MM.
  • This tells the system to bypass the PO requirement and create a vendor invoice directly.

This approach is preferred when no PO exists.

Option 2: Custom Mapping or Enhancement

If your system still attempts MM processing and you want to keep the standard flow:

  • Create a dummy PO automatically to support the invoice posting.
  • Or, implement a user exit / BAdI in IDoc processing to remove or adjust the reference segment (E1EDK02) that points to a non-existent PO.

Option 3: Align Document References

Ensure that the IDoc does not contain a PO number in the segment if one doesn’t exist.
In transaction WE19, test the IDoc by removing the reference segment and see if the system accepts it for FI posting.

Best regards

entry.title: Re: SAP GUI MFA with Microsoft Entra (Part I): Integration with SAP Secure Login Service
entry.content:
Thank you! 

Secure Login Client cannot be integrated directly with Microsoft Entra ID, as Entra ID does not issue the security tokens (Kerberos or X.509) that Secure Login Client requires for authentication to an SAP NetWeaver ABAP system.

entry.title: Re: Getting ICMENOSESSION error while calling SAP S4 webservice from SAP CPI
entry.content:
Well, my personal opinion on this is: you need to be aware, that such "guidelines" are usually not written by people with real low-level technical know-how, but

  1. either by people who live in an ivory tower and want everyone to do what they believe is from a philosophical standpoint the "best" way to do it, even though it may be technically impractical (e.g. performance degradation by a factor 15), or
  2. by people who want to push the technologies their team is responsible for, and discourage technologies that other teams are responsible for. (So basically for "political reasons"...)

All I can say to that is: RFC is an efficient and secure technology, which is still developed further and kept up-to-date with the requirements of modern-day communication scenarios (e.g. the new WebSocket-RFC for secure communication across the internet). If it works fine for you, then use it (regardless of what some nice and shiny marketing powerpoints say), and if it doesn't work fine in the scenario you are trying to achieve, then try the alternatives.

Also you need to know: sometimes you don't have a choice. It depends on the application you want to consume. There are probably hundreds of thousands of different applications in the SAP world (SAP applications, third-party applications and maybe even applications written by yourself). Some applications provide only HTTP-based interfaces, while some applications provide only RFC-based interfaces. In such a case you don't have a choice, you need to use, what the application provides.
Only if the application team went through the effort and exposed both types of interfaces, you can choose which one to use!

entry.title: SAP Solution Manager ITSM: User Access & Automatic Ticket Assignment Configuration

Hi,

We have a couple of configuration-related questions related to our ITSM setup and require your assistance:

  1. User Ticket Visibility (Bucket Restriction):
    • At present, users are able to view tickets raised by others in the system. As per our requirement, we want users to only see:
      1. Tickets they have raised, and
      2. Tickets assigned to them.

  2. Automatic Ticket Assignment from SAP GUI (Double Click on SAP Logo)

  3. Role-Based Access Control for ITSM:

We would appreciate it if you could advise on the steps to meet the above requirements. Looking forward to your prompt response.

Best regards


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP Solution Manager ITSM: User Access & Automatic Ti...
entry.content:
To restrict Incident access with minimal configuration, SM_CRM provides pre-configured widgets which provide pre-filtered and restricted lists of CRM documents. These include "My Messages - Reported by Me", "My Messages - Assigned to Me", etc.

KBA 2735629 has more information. The widgets do not impact the search screens for ITSM (Incident search, etc.). If you need to hide all other Incidents from users, you could change their authorizations so that they cannot edit the UI, remove the options to access those searches in the default views, and add the necessary widgets to the home screen the users default view.

Transaction code SM_WORKCENTER also has a pre-filtered and configured "My Incidents" Fiori app which shows only the Incidents reported by the user. More information about the Fiori apps is available in KBA 2465507.

For more deep control of user access to Incidents, KBA 2479053 can be used to understand and then customize the authorizations for users to restrict access to only CRM documents that they are assigned to.

It may be possible to configure processor auto-assignment using BRF+ rules and a custom PPF action. This could be achieved by adapting the Support Team Determination via BRF+ process. 

If further assistance is required in any of these topics, they are configuration/consulting and can only be supported by a Consulting organization or a Service Request. SAP Support cannot help with these topics as per our policies in note 83020, as they are not bugs or issues in the software.

entry.title: SAP Sustainability Control Tower (SCT) & SAP Environmental Health and Safety (EHS) Integration - SCT
entry.content:
Introduction:

The SAP Sustainability Control Tower (SCT) is a comprehensive solution that enables holistic steering and reporting of sustainability efforts. It applies the SAP Business Technology Platform and core applications, integrating with existing systems to provide unique and effective sustainability solutions.

SAP Environmental Health and Safety management (SAP EHS) has been a key contributor to record, monitor and forecast environmental and social concerns like emissions, people safety, Risk assessments, change management, chemical management, compliance etc.

SAP EHS got abundant information on emission level tracking details and work-related incident details which is now major contributor to SCT.

Let us take a detail look on Sustainability solution SCT and how SAP EHS is integrated with it.

Sustainability Control Tower:

SAP Sustainability Tower (SAP SCT) uses existing system data for the area of sustainability for the fulfilment of ESG reporting requirements and activity management.

Typical data from SAP S/4 HANA, SAP ERP and other SAP solutions which can be used for SAP Sustainability Control Tower:

  • Air emissions, water emissions, CO2 emissions (SAP EHSM, SAP PFM)
  • Waste (SAP EHSM, SAP REA, SAP RDP)
  • Energy (SAP EHS, SAP MM)
  • Accidents, Occupational Health (SAP EHSM)
  • Financial Data, such as sales and pricing (SAP SD)
  • Produced Quantities, Purchased Quantities (SAP PP/PP-PI, MM, Ariba, SAP Concur, SAP Fieldglass)
  • HR such as Headcount (SAP HCM, SuccessFactors)

The goal is to use this data mostly automated, i.e. to automate the data collection required for the ESG-reporting.

 

Benefits of SAP Sustainability Control Tower

  • Integrated solution for sustainability
  • Predefined reporting, KPIs and integration scenarios
  • Automated, up-to-date, and auditable ESG-KPIs
  • Identification of potentials to improve sustainability performance
  • Support of activity management
  • High transparency and efficiency
  • Holistic decision-making based on interrelationships of financial, operational and sustainability performance
  • No technical gaps between operational SAP and sustainability systems
  • Digitization of sustainability

SAP S/4HANA Integration:

This chapter covers the configuration process for data replication in SAP S/4HANA, with two available systems for connection:

  1. SAP S/4HANA On-Premises
  2. SAP S/4HANA Cloud

SAP S/4HANA On-Premise

 

Set up the SAP Cloud Connector, which allows secure communication between the on-premises system and the SAP BTP subaccount.

 

Once the SAP Cloud Connector is set up, you need to configure the HTTP Destination Service on SAP BTP.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud

 

Configure the HTTP destination service on SAP BTP to enable data replication between the SAP S/4HANA Cloud system and the SAP BTP subaccount.

When configuring data replication for SAP S/4HANA, whether it be on-premise or in the cloud, the key difference lies in the process for connecting the SAP S/4HANA system to the SAP BTP subaccount.

 

Set up the Cloud Connector: This serves as a link between applications running on the platform and on-premise systems.

Procedure: SAP ABAP

  1. After installing and starting the Cloud Connector, log on to the administration UI and perform the required configuration to make your Cloud Connector operational.

This includes logging on to the Cloud Connector, setting up connection parameters and an HTTPS proxy, and establishing a connection to the platform.

  1. As soon as the initial setup is complete, the tunnel to the cloud endpoint is open, but no requests are allowed to pass. Go to Cloud to On-Premises and perform the Access Control setup, to specify the back-end systems and resources that can be accessed by your cloud applications (SAP ABAP)

For a back-end system, specify properties such as the Backend Type, the Protocol, the Internal Host, and the Virtual Host.

Note: The Virtual Host is used as the URL property for the destination configuration in the SAP BTP cockpit.

If the Cloud Connector is installed in an environment that is operated by SAP, SAP provides a user that you can add as member in your SAP BTP subaccount and assign the required role.

SAP BTP SUB-ACCOUNT: (Administrator – Any user with access)

When logging in for the first time, the following screen is displayed every time you choose an option from the main menu that requires a configured subaccount.

User Administration:

Create Cloud connector administrator - To edit the password for the Administrator user, choose Configuration from the main menu, tab User Interface, section User Administration.

 

If the internal landscape is protected by a firewall that blocks any outgoing TCP traffic, must specify an HTTPS proxy that the Cloud Connector can use to connect to SAP BTP. Normally, one must use the same proxy settings as those being used by your standard Web browser.

The Cloud Connector needs this proxy for two operations:

  • Download the correct connection configuration corresponding to your subaccount ID in SAP BTP.
  • Establish the SSL tunnel connection from the Cloud Connector user to your SAP BTP subaccount.

The Cloud Connector:

Key points on subaccount connection:

For <Subaccount>, <Subaccount User> and <Password>, enter the values you obtained when you registered your subaccount on SAP BTP.

Note

For a subaccount in the Cloud Foundry environment, you must enter the subaccount ID as <Subaccount>, rather than its actual (technical) name. As <Subaccount User> you must provide your Login E-mail instead of a user ID. The user must be a member of the global account the subaccount belongs to.

You can also add a new subaccount user with the role Cloud Connector Admin in the SAP BTP cockpit and use the new user and password.

The Cloud Connector does not yet support SAP Universal ID. Please use your S-user or P-user credentials for the <subaccount user> and <password> fields instead.

Enter a suitable proxy host from your network and the port that is specified for this proxy. If your network requires an authentication for the proxy, enter a corresponding proxy user and password. You must specify a proxy server that supports SSL communication (a standard HTTP proxy does not suffice).

These settings strongly depend on your specific network setup. If you need more detailed information, please contact your local system administrator.

The Cloud Connector now starts a handshake with SAP BTP and attempts to establish a secure SSL tunnel to the server that hosts the subaccount in which your on-demand applications are running. However, no requests are yet allowed to pass from the cloud side to any of your internal back-end systems. To allow your on-demand applications to access specific internal back-end systems, proceed with the access configuration described in the next section.

If you want to change the description for your Cloud Connector, choose Configuration from the main menu, go to the Cloud tab, section Connector Info and edit the description.

Establish Connections to SAP BTP:

As soon as the initial setup is complete, the tunnel to the cloud endpoint is open, but no requests are allowed to pass until you have performed the Access Control setup.

To manually close (and reopen) the connection to SAP BTP, choose your subaccount from the main menu and select the Disconnect button (or the Connect button to reconnect to SAP BTP).

 

  • The green icon next to Region Host indicates that it is valid and can be reached.
  • If an HTTPS Proxy is configured, its availability is shown the same way. In the screenshot, the grey diamond icon next to HTTPS Proxy indicates that connectivity is possible without proxy configuration.

In case of a timeout or a connectivity issue, these icons are yellow (warning) or red (error), and a tooltip shows the cause of the problem. Initiated By refers to the user that has originally established the tunnel. During normal operations, this user is no longer needed. Instead, a certificate is used to open the connection to a subaccount.

Note

When connected, you can monitor the Cloud Connector also in the Connectivity section of the SAP BTP cockpit. There, you can track attributes like version, description and high availability set up. Every Cloud Connector configured for your subaccount automatically appears in the Connectivity section of the cockpit.

SAP EHS INTEGRATION:

Restrictions include:

  • Only data for the listed substance carbon dioxide equivalent in metric tons is imported.
  • Of the GHG data classifiers in SAP EHS Management, a subset is supported.
  • Only approved, non-preliminary data is imported.
  • The data must be assigned to locations of type Site or Plant. These locations must have a Plant ID. It is used as the identifier for the business location hierarchy of the imported data. Ensure to maintain your SAP Sustainability Control Tower business location hierarchy with the required plants.

Import data from SAP EHS Management

  • Integrate with the SAP EHS Management, environment management solution to import activity data for greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions into SAP Sustainability Control
  • Here is a list of things that can be done with SAP EHSM & SCT integration:
  • 1) Import your recorded GHG emissions from the SAP EHS Management system.
  • 2) Associate your activity data with GHG scopes and sub-categories based on pre-defined classifiers.
  • 3) Assign emission data to business locations in SAP Sustainability Control Tower.
  • 4) Analyse emission data in the View Emission Dashboard app
  • 5) Set and track your emission goals in the SCT Ambitions app.

Import GHG emission data with the Import Data from SAP EHS Management app.

The import uses your SAP S/4HANA configuration for data replication to import calculation results, data collections, and location aggregations using CDS views. Data is imported only for the given time period. The replicated data is transformed and stored into the emissions DPI.

Prerequisites:

  • SAP EHS Management, environment management is active in the SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud system.
  • Connection to SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud.
  • You have the following role assigned to you: SUS_SCT_EHS_Data_Manager.

With this app, you can transfer your emission data from the activities that you have recorded in SAP EHS Management, environment management, to SAP Sustainability Control Tower to use it in the GHG recording for your company.

Specifically, once imported, you can use the associated emission data with the sustainability metrics for GHG emissions across SAP Sustainability Control Tower and, for example, view and analyze the emission data in the View Emission Dashboard app.

You can select if you want to import yearly, monthly, or quarterly data.

Before importing any data, ensure that the data is complete in SAP EHS Management. Review the data after importing it into SAP Sustainability Control Tower.

What Happens with My Data from SAP EHS Management, environment management?

When the emission data from SAP EHS Management is imported, SAP Sustainability Control Tower uses the following information stored in SAP EHS Management to read the data:

  • The standard classifiers you have defined in SAP EHS Management, for example the data classifier Scope 1.
  • User-defined classifiers you have created in SAP Sustainability Control Tower for your activity types, for example, Heating.

These classifiers are then mapped onto the categories available in SAP Sustainability Control Tower to enable further processing of the data.

Example

We have created the following information in SAP EHS Management and want to now use it in SAP Sustainability Control Tower for GHG recording:

  • Standard classifiers: Scope 1, S1 Stationary Combustion
  • User-defined classifiers: Heating
  • Activity: Heating Building 1
  • CO2e Amount: 2,000 t

This information would be mapped to SAP Sustainability Control Tower as follows:

SAP EHS Management, environment management

 

 SAP Sustainability Control Tower     

Activity

CO2e Amount

Classifiers

Activity Type

Scope

Scope Category

Source Category

  

Heating Building 1

2,000 t

Scope 1

(standard)

S1 Stationary Combustion 

(standard)

Heating (user-defined)

Heating

1

1.1 Stationary Combustion

Stationary Combustion

         

One source for issues is a missing standard classifier. For example, for the activity of heating, you have maintained the user-defined classifier Combustion, however no standard classifier exists. In this case, the data will not get imported to avoid incorrect data from getting validated and recorded.

Another source for issues are data conflicts. A data conflict might arise due to mismatches between the used classifiers. For example, for the activity of heating, the standard classifier Scope 1 and the user-defined classifier Truck Transport are maintained, leading to a scope conflict. You can review this data in the Import Data from SAP EHS Management. You have then two possible options to proceed:

  1. Recommended: You can choose to first resolve conflicts in the data and postpone the publication. Once you have corrected the entries, you can re-import the data.
  2. You can start the validation of the successfully imported data and publish it. You can then fix any data with issues and once the next data import runs and is successful, you can start the validation in CPE. You have two options:
    • Update: Existing values are updated with new entries.
    • Create: New entries are entered into the system and stored.

Procedure:

  1. Open the Import Data from SAP EHS Management application from your SAP Fiori launchpad and wait for the connectivity check to complete.
  2. Select a period for importing data from Import Data from SAP EHS Management.
  3. Start the import. The import status is published in the top bar in percent.
  4. Once the import has run, you have different options to proceed depending on the import status:
    • If the import has been Finished successfully, select validate data. You will then be navigated to the CPE environment to validate and publish your data.
    • If the import has been Finished with Issues, you have two options. You can choose to Review Issues first and re-import the data or you can choose to validate data that has been successful and resolve any data with issues later.
    • If the import has Failed, a message will provide you with more details on how to resolve the issue.

Sustainability Control Catalogue:

Below are Master data managed by Sustainability Control Tower with respect to SAP EHSM:

  • Emissions
  • Injury – Injury type and Severity
  • People – Injured Person, Body part, Absence/Restriction

Sustainability metrics can be used for recording actuals and targets. This allows you to understand performance in achieving a set of strategic ambitions for a particular metric. There are more than 40 sustainability metrics in SAP Sustainability Control Tower, such as employee wages and benefits, net investments, and water consumption.

With the app Manage Metrics, you can:

  • Configure sustainability metrics.
  • Set sustainability metrics you'd like to be ambitions for your organization, such as reducing greenhouse gas emissions, work related incidents etc.
  • Upload data to sustainability metrics.

In the overview section, you can find information on:

  • The number of available metrics.
  • The number of metrics that have data uploaded to them.
  • The distribution of metrics according to the areas environmental, social, and corporate governance (ESG), and economic.
  • The number of metrics with set ambitions for your organization.

Prerequisites:

On using SAP S/4HANA Cloud or SAP S/4HANA to upload data in the Manage ESG Data app for sustainability metrics based on financial records, ensure to configure the source system as a destination in your SAP BTP subaccount and that you have uploaded the required master data to SAP Sustainability Control Tower.

  • Sustainability Control Tower: Sustainability Metrics for EHS:
  • Gross GHG Emissions – Scope 1
  • Gross GHG Emissions – Scope 2 Market/Location Based
  • Gross GHG Emissions – Scope 3 Market/Location Based
  • Incidents per millions of hours worked
  • Work Related Fatalities
  • Work Related Incidents

Procedure: Administrator (User with Access to SMC):

In Sustainability Metric Catalogue, Sustainability metrics are uploaded with respect to status (data provided/no data), Area (Social/ Governance/ Economic/Environmental) and Ambition (Ambition set/ No Ambition).

 

Incidents per millions of hours worked:

The value of this sustainability metric represents the number of work-related incidents per million hours worked. It includes health and safety process data on incidents that have happened to workers.

Maintaining strong standards of health, safety, and labor rights can improve employee productivity and operational efficiency and enhance employee well-being. Working proactively in these areas of business will help identify and mitigate potential risks and it is increasingly required by law. Mental health and emotional wellbeing, as components of overall worker health and safety, are becoming increasingly important in driving innovation and delivering goods and services that are reliant on human capital. Organizations that invest in non-occupational medical and healthcare services would demonstrate to their employees that they care for and look after them. This would result in a purposeful, resilient, and growth-oriented workforce.

Calculation Methodology

Incidents per million hours worked = INJURY_TOTAL / (HOURS_WORKED /1000000)

Injury count = INJURY_TOTAL

Total hours worked = HOURS_WORKED

DPI to be Used

DPI - Data is uploaded using CSV files (also referred to as 'flat files') through the appropriate Data Provider Interface (DPI). DPIs define the structure of the data to be uploaded, and act as an interface to the system, allowing you to upload your data.

The sustainability metric "incidents per million hours worked" uses the following DPI:

  • Injury - measure "Injured" (ID_MEASURE: INJ_INJURED_PERSON) is used
  • People - measure "Hours worked" (ID_MEASURE: PPL_HOURS_WORKED) is used

Dimensions Related to Actuals

The calculation of the sustainability metric "incidents per million hours worked" does not use any domain-specific dimension.

Targets

Like other sustainability metrics supporting standard organizational structures, the data for the sustainability metric "incidents per million hours worked" is uploaded directly as ratio per period - per the organizational unit targets. These targets can only be uploaded for a defined leading hierarchy.

Work Related Fatalities:

The value of this sustainability metric represents the number of work-related fatalities. It includes health and safety process data on incidents (fatalities) that have happened to workers.

The metric describes number of fatalities as a result of work-related injury and high-consequence work-related injuries (including fatalities only). Maintaining strong standards of health, safety, and labor rights can improve employee productivity and operational efficiency and enhance employee well-being. Working proactively in these areas of business will help identify and mitigate potential risks and it is increasingly required by law. Mental health and emotional wellbeing, as components of overall worker health and safety, are becoming increasingly important in driving innovation and delivering goods and services that are reliant on human capital. Organizations that invest in non-occupational medical and healthcare services would demonstrate to their employees that they care for and look after them. This would result in a purposeful, resilient, and growth-oriented workforce.

Calculation Methodology

Number of fatalities = This calculation limits the measure incidents count by dimension injury severity (ID_INJURY_SEVERITY), Values: Fatal (3) where incidents count = INJURY_COUNT

DPI to be Used

The sustainability metric "work-related fatalities" uses the following DPI: Injury - measure injured (ID_MEASURE: INJ_INJURED_PERSON)

Dimensions Related to Actuals

The calculation of the sustainability metric "work-related fatalities" uses the domain-specific dimension ID_INJURY_SEVERITY.

Work-Related Incidents:

The value of this sustainability metric represents the number of work-related incidents. It includes health and safety process data on incidents that have happened to workers.

Maintaining strong standards of health, safety, and labor rights can improve employee productivity and operational efficiency and enhance employee well-being. Working proactively in these areas of business will help identify and mitigate potential risks, and it is increasingly required by law. Mental health and emotional wellbeing, as components of overall worker health and safety, are becoming increasingly important in driving innovation and delivering goods and services that are reliant on human capital. Organizations that invest in non-occupational medical and healthcare services would demonstrate to their employees that they care for and look after them. This would result in a purposeful, resilient and growth-oriented workforce.

Calculation Methodology

Number of incidents = This calculation limits the measure incidents count by dimension injury severity (ID_INJURY_SEVERITY), Values: all (1, 2 and 3) where incidents count = INJURY_COUNT

DPI to be Used

The sustainability metric "work-related incidents" uses the following DPI: Injury - measure injured (ID_MEASURE: INJ_INJURED_PERSON)

Dimensions Related to Actuals

The calculation of the sustainability metric "work-related incidents" uses the domain-specific dimension ID_INJURY_SEVERITY.

Conclusion:

The journey towards sustainability is fraught with numerous challenges that organizations must navigate. To effectively manage and overcome these challenges, companies need robust tools and systems. One such essential tool is the SAP Sustainability Control Tower, a core component of SAP Cloud for Sustainable Enterprises solutions. With an effective integration to SAP EHS and other existing SAP submodules, SAP SCT is now a game changer.

Thank You!

 

Regards,

Sai Santhosh. B

entry.title: How to Return or Cancel the Picked Stock in Outbound Delivery in SAP WM
entry.content:
In warehouse operations, outbound deliveries often go through picking and transfer order (TO) confirmations before goods issue. But what happens when a mistake occurs after the pick is confirmed - such as product damage during packing or picking from the wrong bin?

Let’s consider a business scenario:

A warehouse associate confirms a Transfer Order (TO) for outbound delivery. However, the goods are later found to be damaged or misplaced. The warehouse supervisor now needs to return the picked stock to the storage bin and restart the picking process.

SAP provides a standard functionality for such cases using Transaction Code LT0G (Return Delivery to Stock). This blog explains step-by-step how to handle this real-time challenge efficiently within the Warehouse Management (WM) module.

Step-by-Step Process to Reverse or Cancel Picked Stock in Outbound Delivery:

Step 1: Create Sales Order (Transaction: VA01)

Step 2: Create Outbound Delivery (App: Create Outbound Delivery/Transaction: VL01NO)

You can create the outbound delivery:

  • With reference to a Sales Order (e.g., SO 145), or

  • Without order reference (manually via VL01NO).

✅ After saving the delivery, observe that the WM status will be A, A (Not yet started).

Outbound Delivery Created 80000165

Step 3: Create Transfer Order (TO) (Transaction: LT03)

From the outbound delivery screen, navigate to Create Transfer Order.

 

 

Use the Stock Removal Foreground to manually choose the source bin.

Step 4: Confirm Transfer Order (Transaction: LT12)

 

  • Use Tcode: LT12 or the relevant menu path to confirm the TO.

  • 🔒 Note: Once TO is confirmed, cancellation is not allowed directly.

Step 5: Check Document Flow (Transaction: VA03)
  • After confirmation, WM status changes to C, C (Completed).

Stock Check in tcode LS26

Note: As stated once TO is created and confirmed, it cannot be cancelled/reversed, but here let us assume that the product was damaged after packing and the WM user wants to cancel this picking process. Here comes the SAP standard functionality Return Delivery to Stock (Tcode: LT0G)

Step 6: Handle Reversal (Transaction: LT0G)

Use LT0G when goods need to be returned after TO confirmation.

Enter:

  • Warehouse Number

  • Delivery Number

  • Movement Type: 999

Choose the Return option

 

  • Putaway Forward – manually pick the bin

  • Putaway Background – system determines the bin

  • Return to Stock – sends stock back to the source bin

Once posted, stock will be updated under Pick qty and TO will be created awaiting for confirmation.

Step 7: Confirm the Reversal TO

After executing LT0G, a new TO is created for reversal.

  • Use Tcode: LT12 again to confirm this new TO.

  • The stock will now be moved back into the storage bin.

Once confirmed, the picked quantity will be moved back to the source storage bin.

After confirmation, WM status changes to A, A (Not Yet Picked, WM TO Required).

Step 8: Restart the Picking Process

 

  • Repeat the picking process by referring to steps 3 and 4.

  • You may now select a different bin depending on the scenario.

Once picking process is completed, the WM status changes to C, C (Completed)

Step 9: Perform post Goods Issue (PGI) (Transaction: VA02)

  • Open the outbound delivery in change mode (VL02N).

  • Execute Post Goods Issue.

📦 Once PGI is done:

  • Stock is removed from the storage bin.

  • Goods movement status updates to C.

 

Step 10: Check Document Flow (Transaction: VA03)

Use Tcode: VL03N to review document flow for:

  • Delivery creation

  • TO confirmation

  • LT0G reversal

  • PGI completion

If you found this guide helpful, don’t miss my other SAP MM blogs covering real-world automation, configuration tips, and best practices for procurement and inventory management.

Click to view other SAP MM Blogs - SAP Community

Regards,

Leelamohan

entry.title: HU from EWM to ERP HU managed Sloc creates multiple record in VEKP and this lead to issues
entry.content:
HU is transfrerred from storage location managed in EWM to a storage location in ERP managed with HU and WM. in VEKP multiple records for same EXIDV are created. When GI is posted HU remains in status 0050 and when the HU is received in the HU managed storage location, a new record is created in VEKP. 

Standard transaction like LM18 reads the wrong record in VEKP

entry.title: Re: Verwendung von Testplänen in SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Hallo Daniel,

wir nutzen Testpläne der SAP CALM aktiv im Rahmen eines S4-Transformationsprojektes. Das Feedback von unseren Tester ist überwiegend positiv.

Aktuell haben wir die Herausforderung, dass wir vorhandene Testpläne in den Status "Beendet" überführt haben. Leider mussten wir feststellen, dass alle Testfälle eines abgeschlossenen Testplans weiterhin ausgeführt werden können. Ist dieses so gewollt oder machen wir etwas falsch? Aus unsere Sicht insbesondere aus Sicht der Abnahme durch einen WP sollte diese nicht gehen, da sonst Zwischenergebnisse verfälscht werden. Gern können wir uns die Herausforderung bei uns im System anschauen ... 

M. Endig

entry.title: Reconciliation Balances on the fly translation to euro
entry.content:
Hello!

We are testing the scope item Intercompany Reconciliation and I would just like to ask what does the 'on the fly translation to euro' mean?

When we use this after matching (Manage Assignments app) the balance is zero, which is perfect. But when we use other currencies like transaction currency or global currency the balances are a mess. Why is that?

It was a transaction between a German (EUR) and a Romanian (RON) company code.
